sourceMovie.title: Alien: Romulus
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Alien: Romulus works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Alien
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Alien: Romulus clicked for you, Alien (1979) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Alien: Romulus?
faq.answer: Try Alien, Life, The Thing—each shares DNA with Alien: Romulus while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Alien: Romulus?
faq.answer: Alien: Romulus (2024) is best described as Horror, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Alien: Romulus part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Alien: Romulus Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
